$comment: Breakpoint tokens. Semantic, not device names. Use the named breakpoint that matches the *reason* for the change, not the device you tested on. Code source of truth: packages/ui/src/tokens/tokens.json (semantic.container) and packages/ui/tailwind.config.ts (screens).

base.usage: The default. Styles without a breakpoint prefix target this — the smallest supported viewport.
base.notes: Smallest first-class target is 375 x 667 (iPhone SE). 320px must not break but isn't optimized for.

sm.usage: Large phones / phablets. Two-column lists, expanded toolbars on tall phones.

md.usage: Tablets, foldables. Side panels appear, tables can show all columns, modals widen.
md.notes: Tailwind: md: prefix. Touch is still the assumed input here.

lg.usage: Small laptops. Pointer input is assumed at this size and above; hover affordances may appear here.
lg.notes: Tailwind: lg: prefix. Density may step down by one stack-size at lg+.

xl.usage: Standard desktop. Three- and four-column dashboards, persistent sidebar.

2xl.usage: Wide desktop / external monitor. Avoid filling all the width — content max-widths apply.
2xl.notes: Tailwind: 2xl: prefix. Container widths cap below the raw viewport at this size.

container.$description: Maximum content widths for page-level surfaces. Pages reference these — they never invent a max-width.

sm.usage: Reading surfaces: marketing body sections, blog posts, help articles.

md.usage: Narrow product surfaces: settings pages, single-column forms.

lg.usage: Default product container: dashboards on small laptops.

xl.usage: Wide product container: dashboards on desktop.

2xl.usage: Reserved for marketing pages and content galleries.
2xl.notes: Tailwind: max-w-container-2xl. Almost never the right answer in product.
